schema_rule: panel_id selects one of three simulated regimes; the swept knob for that panel and the fast time axis vary row-to-row, together with the single simulated observable (population). All scalar constants of the run live once in metadata.json, not repeated per row.

columns.name: panel_id
columns.description: 0 ring-down map (single pulse, drive freq swept), 1 gap sweep (two pulses, phase2=0, gap swept, at FREQ_STAR), 2 phase sweep (two pulses, fixed gap, phase2 swept, at FREQ_STAR).

columns.name: drive_frequency_GHz
columns.description: Microwave drive frequency. Swept across 2.0-5.0 GHz in panel 0; held at FREQ_STAR in panels 1 and 2.

columns.name: amp1
columns.description: Amplitude of the first (or only) drive pulse. Constant at 0.12 throughout.

columns.name: amp2
columns.description: Amplitude of the second drive pulse (0.12 in panels 1,2; 0 in panel 0 where there is no second pulse).

columns.name: pulse1_ns
columns.description: Duration of the first pulse. Constant (200 ns).

columns.name: gap_ns
columns.description: Idle gap after pulse 1 before pulse 2. Swept 0-800 ns in panel 1; fixed at 150 ns in panel 2; 0 in panel 0 (single pulse).

columns.name: pulse2_ns
columns.description: Duration of the second pulse (200 ns in panels 1,2; 0 in panel 0).

columns.name: phase2_rad
columns.description: Relative phase of the second pulse. Swept 0-2*pi in panel 2; 0 elsewhere. Decides whether the second pulse's response adds to or cancels the ring-down of the first.

columns.name: timestamp_ns
columns.description: Simulation time within the trace. Identical grid for every trace, so traces are directly comparable.

columns.name: population
columns.description: Collective excitation <sigma+ sigma-> of the N=2 TLS pair, computed by the Lindblad solver. Non-negative. The sole output.

computable_not_stored.note: population is the only observable stored here.
